programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The anglers follow a lead to a remote river in Greenland, believed to hold huge Arctic char. They also investigate a myth of Atlantic salmon occupying a lone river

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Long Island medium Theresa Caputo asks Wayde and Brett to build her a mermaid tank and a koi pond

programme.title: Imagine
programme.sub-title: Tracey Emin: Where Do You Draw the Line?
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the artist over the course of 12 months to chart her creative process, talking about her life to date and how she sees the rest of her career. Tracey discusses her troubled early years in Margate and the series of breakthroughs in the 1990s that saw her become a leading light of the Young British Artists, featuring career-defining work like My Bed and her embroidered tent Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995. Contributors include Nicholas Serota, Jay Jopling, Maria Balshaw and David Dawson

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: Edvard Munchs work has long inspired Tracey Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental nine-metre high bronze sculpture The Mother. This documentary charts the affinities between Munchs art and Emins, exploring the ways that this godfather of expressionism resonates with one of Britains pre-eminent artists

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of Charlestown in Cornwall, revealing how the villagess namesake Charles Rashleigh transformed the local trade and commerce from fishing and distribution of pilchards, to minerals exported by a hand-built port with pioneering technology. Ben looks at how Charles commissioned renowned engineer John Smeaton to create man-made lakes and hand-dug ponds, and explores their impact on the villages prosperity

programme.title: Wainwright Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury follows in guidebook writer Alfred Wainwrights footsteps and walks across northern England, linking three national parks. She begins at St Bees Head on the coast of Cumbria, and has to deal with appalling weather conditions as she tackles her first Lakeland valley

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson pays a visit to the beautifully preserved village of Arnside, sitting just outside the Lake District on the banks of the River Kent. Once serving the port of Milnthorpe, the village expanded with the arrival of ship carpenter John Crossfield, leading to its expansion in the early 1800s as part of the boat-building industry. Ben talks to Victorian industry expert Dr Stephen Caunce and meets a descendant of the Crossfields family, talking about the villages tourist success and architectural history

programme.title: Wainwright Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury continues to follow in the footsteps of guidebook writer Alfred Wainwright, and tackles three mountain ranges and valleys in the Lake District. Her journey includes an epic 17-mile walk that takes in the length of Haweswater and the highest peak of her expedition

programme.title: The Birds
programme.desc: Wildlife film-maker Martin Dohrn contemplates the end of his career as he points the camera towards the dramatic dance between a million seabirds and a single hunting falcon. As he uncovers new meaning in the mesmeric murmurations across the coast of Norfolk, he reflects on his career behind the camera

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: Witnessing a natural spectacle that happens each year in the heart of southern Sweden, when 10,000 cranes stop off at a special lake while migrating northwards. As the spring sunshine begins to melt the snow and ice, the birds can be seen dancing as they gather around the waters edge

programme.title: Secrets of Size: Atoms to Supergalaxies
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jim Al-Khalili explores the universe at the smallest scales, from tiny atoms to vast galaxies. The first episode examines objects too small to glimpse with the naked eye, and reveals how nanomaterials such as graphene are revolutionising engineering, medicine, computing, electronics and environmental science

programme.title: Daniela Nardini Remembers - This Life
programme.desc: Looking back on the beloved series from the 1990s, dubbed the antidote to Friends that depicted the warts-and-all life of young people in London, Daniela reflects on the role that changed her life and the overall experience of filming the drama that had a generation hooked

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: First episode of the drama charting the lives of five flat-sharing lawyers in London. No-nonsense law graduate Anna goes for a job at a barristers office - only to find an old college flame on the interview panel who offers her the position in return for a repeat performance of their last one-night stand. Starring Daniela Nardini, Andrew Lincoln, Jack Davenport, Amita Dhiri and Jason Hughes

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Miles is seduced by a woman facing fraud charges, ruining Annas hopes of renewing their romance. Eggs dissatisfaction with his new responsibilities is deepened by Warrens apparent confidence, but the arrival of his cousin threatens to shatter the facade. Jason Hughes, Andrew Lincoln and Jack Davenport star

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Mark Chapman narrates an exploration of Britains complicated relationship with alcohol during the latter half of the 20th century. The programme charts how conventions surrounding the drinking habits of men and women changed from the 1940s onwards, as the largely masculine culture of pubs and working mens clubs began to recede, and also discovers the influence foreign travel and advertising had on the types of beverage that were popular in the UK

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson pays a visit to the beautifully preserved village of Arnside, sitting just outside the Lake District on the banks of the River Kent. Once serving the port of Milnthorpe, the village expanded with the arrival of ship carpenter John Crossfield, leading to its expansion in the early 1800s as part of the boat-building industry. Ben talks to Victorian industry expert Dr Stephen Caunce and meets a descendant of the Crossfields family, talking about the villages tourist success and architectural history

programme.title: Wainwright Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury continues to follow in the footsteps of guidebook writer Alfred Wainwright, and tackles three mountain ranges and valleys in the Lake District. Her journey includes an epic 17-mile walk that takes in the length of Haweswater and the highest peak of her expedition

programme.title: The Birds
programme.desc: Wildlife film-maker Martin Dohrn contemplates the end of his career as he points the camera towards the dramatic dance between a million seabirds and a single hunting falcon. As he uncovers new meaning in the mesmeric murmurations across the coast of Norfolk, he reflects on his career behind the camera

programme.title: Secrets of Size: Atoms to Supergalaxies
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jim Al-Khalili explores the universe at the smallest scales, from tiny atoms to vast galaxies. The first episode examines objects too small to glimpse with the naked eye, and reveals how nanomaterials such as graphene are revolutionising engineering, medicine, computing, electronics and environmental science

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits Alnmouth, Northumberland, which was founded in the mid-12th century after the barons of nearby Alnwick obtained a charter from King John to build a new village. It had a market and a small port and the village flourished - so much so that by the mid-1700s, Alnmouths grain export started to play an integral role in feeding the growing English population

programme.title: Wainwright Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: A third of the way through Alfred Wainwrights route across northern England, Julia Bradbury reaches the Eden Valley. The bustling town of Kirkby Stephen marks the beginning of her climb over the Pennines, but wet weather makes for a challenging journey

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the Normans expansion toward southern Europe and the Middle East in the 11th century. Southern Italy and Sicily were conquered and a prosperous kingdom flourished on the island, which became an important centre of medieval culture and an example of harmony between religions. However, Pope Urban IIs call for holy war against the Muslims, to win Jerusalem back, led to the Normans embarking on the First Crusade

programme.title: Cabaret (1972)
programme.desc: A flighty nightclub singer in 1930s Berlin is drawn into a love triangle with a sensitive English writer and an aristocratic German, as the Nazi party grows in might and the country heads inexorably toward war. Oscar-winning musical directed and choreographed by Bob Fosse, starring Liza Minnelli, Joel Grey, Michael York and Helmut Griem

programme.title: New York, New York (1977)
programme.desc: An egotistical saxophonist starts a turbulent love affair with an up-and-coming vocalist at the end of World War Two as they both struggle to make it in the Big Band era. Martin Scorseses musical drama, starring Robert De Niro, Liza Minnelli, Lionel Stander, Barry Primus and Georgie Auld

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits Alnmouth, Northumberland, which was founded in the mid-12th century after the barons of nearby Alnwick obtained a charter from King John to build a new village. It had a market and a small port and the village flourished - so much so that by the mid-1700s, Alnmouths grain export started to play an integral role in feeding the growing English population

programme.desc: A third of the way through Alfred Wainwrights route across northern England, Julia Bradbury reaches the Eden Valley. The bustling town of Kirkby Stephen marks the beginning of her climb over the Pennines, but wet weather makes for a challenging journey

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the Normans expansion toward southern Europe and the Middle East in the 11th century. Southern Italy and Sicily were conquered and a prosperous kingdom flourished on the island, which became an important centre of medieval culture and an example of harmony between religions. However, Pope Urban IIs call for holy war against the Muslims, to win Jerusalem back, led to the Normans embarking on the First Crusade

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. Omar is in denial, and Toby is distracted by Abbys dangerous games. Violence erupts on the hockey pitch, and the students bright futures feel further away than ever

programme.title: Crimewatch Live
programme.desc: The capture of a gang trafficking £7 million of heroin across the UK. Plus, an appeal for help to solve a rape from 1987. Presented by Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy Walsh takes a trip down memory lane in Nottinghamshire, where an old-fashioned fireplace has him feeling nostalgic. Hes also in Stoke, where a bungalow proves to be a great project for a new development duo to tackle together. Dion Dublin is in Newcastle-under-Lyme, where theres potential to go up into the loft to create more space

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian presents from Naseby in Northamptonshire, where experts Kate Bliss and David Harper offer up their expertise to assist husband and wife Shaun and Lea for the blues and father and daughter Dave and Leanne for the reds. Danny also has a mystery item for fairgoers to solve - a miniature jolly policeman in silver plate. But what was it used for?

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: A few months on, Bear revisits some of the people who came to the wild for his help, to resolve conflict in their lives and relationships. How have things have changed for them?

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr fight against their maximalist taste to create a calming Balearic bedroom. The pair settle on an all-white, exposed natural brick feel. To nail their vision, the duo visit BandB rivals and local friends Ian and Andrews Ibiza-inspired holiday let, where bespoke storage and an innovative approach to the use of plaster get their creative juices flowing. Swapping out the boiler suits for Lycra, the pair later scope out Moclins famous cycling routes for future guests for the BandB and later still head to the Strait of Gibraltar for the trip of a lifetime

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams helps a London-based couple escape from the hustle and bustle of the city and find a new home in the countryside of either Somerset or Devon. She also meets some former Londoners who converted a cowshed into a self-sufficient family home

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea teams up with carpenter Jen to save a beloved spider slide for a community centre in Dunblane, near Stirling, hoping to make it safe for the next generation to enjoy. Will Kirk collect the Appin Chalice, a Jacobite relic thought to have been used to give communion before the Battle of Culloden in 1746, while Dom learns the secrets behind hand-blown glass paperweights in Crieff, Perth and Kinross

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz, which sees contestants Manisha, Malachy, Ciara, Marc and Chanel race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Ed Gamble

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Alex Jones present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Josh and Sandra join Denise and Kim for a drink in the Albert as he searches for answers about Jasmine, but Denise soon puts her foot in it by mentioning Zoe. Lauren and Peter meet a mum whose baby is also visually impaired, to discuss a specialist nursery, but the pair bristle when they realise the cost. Elsewhere, Billy grows concerned about Beas obsession with his family after speaking to Harvey, and Suki comes to blows with Penny and Vinny about the pregnancy

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Gary from north Wales brings in an electric guitar that once belonged to his father Eddie Clayton - who once played in a skiffle group with none other than a pre-Beatles Ringo Starr. The instrument is now damaged and no longer working, so restorer Julyan Wallis gets to work. Other items are an exquisite bangle worn by the owners grandmother, who left Guyana for the UK in 1962 as part of the Windrush generation, a familys rediscovered set of Kent peg tiles and a leather tallit bag - a Jewish family heirloom passed down through generations when a boy celebrates his bar mitzvah

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Tracey and Lauren, one of the five ambulance crews covering the Yorkshire coastal towns of Scarborough and Whitby, are kept busy over the bank holiday weekend. Barnsley couple Alan and Jean have travelled to their holiday home, only for Jean to have a fall - and it soon becomes clear to the medics she is suffering from life-threatening sepsis. In ambulance control, one handler tries to arrange help for a man who has had an accident while working, while another is dealing with a 999 call from the daughter of an elderly woman who is choking in the remote village of Sinnington

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates, with viewers sending in their questions via text, email and social media

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Action from the last-16 second-leg ties, including Liverpool v Galatasaray at Anfield and Tottenham Hotspur v Atletico Madrid at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Reds lost away to Gala in the league phase, but will have expected to do enough to progress this evening in front of their own fans. Spurs have struggled domestically this term, but much like last season when they won the UEFA Europa League, they have performed much better in continental competition

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material. programme.title: Crimewatch Live
programme.desc: How police jailed three organised crime groups in one swoop for their roles in a £53 million drug empire. Plus, a call for information on the murder of trainee nursery assistant Camille Gordon, who was killed in Soho, London

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a large home in Barry in south Wales, a terrace in Nuneaton and a quirky one-bedroom flat in a Victorian former schoolhouse on the Isle of Wight

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.desc: A behind the scenes look at the making of the programme, the survival expert shares the ideas and planning involved in the shows creation and how the tasks were filmed, whatever the weather. With input from the mediators, therapist and psychologist who guided those involved through their wild experience

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr reach the last room of the Andalusian townhouse, deciding to go all-out designing a Moorish-themed bar. Amanda refuses to hold back and is ready and willing to bring in some opulence, keen to drench the room in patterned wallpaper and fabulous gold touches. Taking a break from the property, the pair head to a traditional tea house in the oldest neighbourhood in Granada - and get more than they bargained for when theyre treated to a surprise belly dance performance. Later, they reveal their completed labour of love with a big farewell party for their friends and family.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse helps a pair of newlyweds from London swap the chaos of the city for the serenity of the Surrey countryside so they can finally get the dog theyve been dreaming of. programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Isla, Wayne, Mwari, Colm and Laura race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

She then heads to Ko Phangan, an exotic island famed for its spirituality and beauty. For Zara, this is paradise - but Lucio, a Brit who built a burgeoning cannabis business here while the drug was decriminalised, reveals that navigating the local law isnt always easy

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.sub-title: West Ham United v Manchester United 18.03
programme.desc: West Ham United v Manchester United (Kick-off 7.15pm). Coverage of the top-flight match at Chigwell Construction Stadium in Dagenham. Goals from Elisabeth Terland and Dominique Janssen inspired United to a 2-1 victory when these teams met at Leigh Sports Village last December, which at the time halted a two game-losing streak for the Red Devils and moved them back up to third in the table, while the defeat saw the Irons stay second from bottom

programme.title: Dragons Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Steven Bartlett are joined by beauty mogul Jenna Meek as they grill the latest business hopefuls. Pitching to the Dragons this time - a founder from Northern Ireland presents her snack range designed to unleash the natural power of oats. A father-and-son duo unveil their lockdown-born DIY gadget. programme.desc: Zara McDermott experiences Ko Phangans famous Full Moon Party, but beneath the cheap cocktails, music and fire shows, theres danger lurking as the medical team treat a possible drink-spiking victim. She then travels to Phuket and experiences a taste of Thailands luxury lifestyle. But she also learns how dream holidays can quickly go wrong, hearing the case of a British holiday-maker who died on the island - and whose family is still searching for answers

programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team head to Norway, where they follow the tracks of Europes largest cat, the lynx, and encounter Arctic foxes

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.sub-title: Dogs and Wolves, Norway, UK and Romania
programme.desc: Steve Backshall takes a close look at dogs and their distant relatives, wolves. He heads to the Arctic Circle where a pack of huskies show him a thing or two about stamina. Steve is then rescued by an avalanche dog whose incredible nose sniffs him out from deep beneath the snow. Finally he comes face-to-face with one of his favourite animals, the wolf.

programme.desc: The agnostic presenter goes in search of salvation in Americas Bible Belt, where Christian TV channel host Marcus Lamb invites him to be born again live on air as a highlight of his show

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Weird Weekends
programme.desc: The intrepid reporter delves into Americas porn industry, meeting veteran actress Sharon Mitchell, who featured in the infamous 1970s blue movie Debbie Does Dallas. Later, he poses nude for a talent agent and gets some first-hand advice from an adult-movie star

programme.title: The Martian (2015)
programme.desc: A manned mission to Mars is abruptly abandoned, with one crew member left for dead. However, the astronaut has survived and faces a series of challenges - to contact Earth and arrange his rescue and to survive for the years it will take for a spacecraft to reach him on resources intended to last the crew a month. Ridley Scotts sci-fi drama based on the novel by Andy Weir, starring Matt Damon, Jessica Chastain, Kristen Wiig and Chiwetel Ejiofor

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Weird Weekends
programme.desc: The agnostic presenter goes in search of salvation in Americas Bible Belt, where Christian TV channel host Marcus Lamb invites him to be born again live on air as a highlight of his show

But she also learns how dream holidays can quickly go wrong, hearing the case of a British holiday-maker who died on the island - and whose family is still searching for answers

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: In the early hours a frantic teenage girl calls 999 to say that her friend has been attacked and stabbed in a car park. Police rush to the scene, where they find a 16-year-old with a life-threatening wound to his stomach. While his life hangs in the balance, West Midlands Polices homicide team get to work, with forensic investigators discovering two discarded weapons at the scene - a kitchen knife and a machete

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Lord Sugar tasks the candidates with making a demo for their own virtual reality fitness brand that packs a punch and scores big on investments. Both teams work up a sweat but in the boardroom theres nowhere to run

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the latest task, and the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: A benefits assessor comes to evaluate Freyas needs and determine how many hours of PA financial assistance she will receive. Jo unexpectedly turns up at the venue for Janes book launch to try to make some progress with a clearly wounded Levi

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: The mathematician explores the link between a controversial AI algorithm and Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. There were allegations the company used artificial intelligence to deny insurance claims from vulnerable patients, while Mangione himself studied AI at university. Hannah travels to the US to meet a woman who lost her husband and claims it happened after he was denied healthcare by an algorithm, and she also speaks to a UnitedHealthcare insider who reveals her concerns about their use of AI

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: The adventurer tries to help couple Lisha and Jenna, whose different communication styles mean they are constantly fighting. They hope that time in the wild can help them repair their relationship

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea lends a hand with an 8,000-litre paint job and fixes a department stores pneumatic tube system. Will Kirk brings a shattered wedding bowl to colleague Kirsten Ramsay for a delicate restoration

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps globetrotter Sabiha find an Argyll and Bute retreat, with a line-up that includes a magical home on an island, just a short ferry ride from the mainland. He also meets a couple who made their own Argyll and Bute dream come true when they moved to a wonderful lochside croft

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Abandoned by their dominant males, the Xudum pride comes under siege from more intruder lions, and the mums with young cubs must link up with the rest of the pride for protection. Leopard mum Lediba struggles after a lion steals her kill. To feed her cub, she is forced to make a dangerous move. Pobe is teaching cheetah cubs Kea and Bo to hunt, but when a hyena pack arrives, their survival instincts are pushed to the limit. With predators all around, every mother must fight harder than ever to keep her young alive

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame
programme.desc: Sue Perkins hosts the high-stakes contest, which sees the five remaining players from the first heat return to the Great Hall to battle it out for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Godfather fan Clive is in Sicily, where he visits the village that Francis Ford Coppola used to film a wedding in his classic movie The Godfather. He also joins geologists working on Mount Etna to predict its next eruption and heads to Modica to learn how to make a chocolate bar from an ancient recipe

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The females take centre stage as competition hots up between the families. Lioness Kali and her sister are enjoying being together with their cubs. Hunting is much easier together and life is good. But their happiness is short-lived when male lion Sefu begins to stalk them. Cheetah and new mum Kike moves to the area and must quickly learn to protect her cubs from predators, while Bakari the baboon struggles to raise the baby he rescued when its mother was killed and desperately tries to find a female to help him

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel takes the programme to Blackpool, where seaside treasures await him. Discovering the predecessor of This Is Your Lifes big red book, he also comes across a signed programme from the 1936 cup tie between Bolton and Blackpool, an unusual medal awarded for selling vacuum cleaners in the 1930s, and a rose-patterned Staffordshire mug that provides a clue to its famous former owner

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Michael Baggott value items in Cirencester, Gloucestershire, including a 17th-century wine bottle and costume jewellery by a leading fashion designer. Paul Martin also visits Kelmscott House, the former home of William Morris, father of the arts and crafts movement

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Backshall, Steve Pemberton, Sam Quek and Rachel Riley test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts a quiz show in which he and Traitors star Paul Gorton test Susan Calman, Max Fosh and Noreen Khan on their knowledge of the UK

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from Wales serve their mains and desserts to judge Tommy Banks. Including guinea fowl in tribute to Welsh-Zambian director Rungano Nyoni and a dish inspired by Snow White that uses frozen snow

programme.title: Britains Biggest Warship
programme.sub-title: Out with the Old, In with the New
programme.desc: The crew of HMS Queen Elizabeth completes the vessels sea trials, testing an innovative new firefighting system for the first time and marking the 100th anniversary of a plane being successfully landed on a moving ship. The carrier returns to Portsmouth, which will be its home port for the next 50 years, while the pilots of its fighter jets go to the US for additional training

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: When Britain and the US refuse to negotiate with Islamic State, James Foley pays the ultimate price at the hands of Jihadi John. After the execution is streamed worldwide, John Cantlie reappears in a very different propaganda video. He is alive and apparently reporting on behalf of the extremists, forcing the world to question what side he is on and how far he must go to stay alive

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: The singer and his nephew take a trip along the Atlantic coast, starting in Cork. They go foraging for edible plants and face off in the ancient sport of road bowling along the back roads of the port town of Castletown Berehaven. They also visit an enormous rewilding project area and on the Beara Peninsula and the countrys first Tibetan Buddhist Temple and Retreat Centre

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Faisal Islam

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Investigations into social and political issues affecting Northern Ireland

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: After an intense briefing on the influencer campaign from the Olivias, Freyas misgivings about the whole endeavour are at an all-time high. Jo is in less of a celebratory mood and is stunned to find a surprise visitor at the hen do

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: The BBCs world affairs editor reflects on the weeks major global news stories, talking to local experts from around the world to go behind the headlines

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs from Wales serve up their canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. The dishes are judged by Michelin-starred chef Tommy Banks, and include two celeriac dishes, one honouring Under Milk Wood and Richard Burton and the other TE Lawrence. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya begins in Germanys Bavarian mountains, paragliding over Neuschwanstein Castle before travelling to meet the mayor of Kallstadt, the ancestral home of the Trump family, and heading to Ramstein, Americas largest airbase in Europe Ramstein. Crossing to Spain, Katya begins in the region of Catalonia, discovering how its strong nationalist identity ties into the legacy of dictator Francisco Franco

programme.title: Dunblane: How Britain Banned Handguns
programme.desc: On March 13, 1996, Thomas Hamilton walked into the gym hall in Dunblane Primary School in Stirlingshire and shot dead 16 pupils and their teacher Gwen Mayor, seriously injuring 14 other pupils and two teachers. This documentary follows the campaign to ban private handgun ownership in the UK in the wake of the massacre, revealing how a group of ordinary people forced the government to ensure the tragedy could not happen again

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: A few months on, Bear revisits some of the people who came to the wild for his help, to resolve conflict in their lives and relationships. How have things have changed for them?

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea teams up with carpenter Jen to save a beloved spider slide for a community centre in Dunblane, near Stirling, hoping to make it safe for the next generation to enjoy. Will Kirk collect the Appin Chalice, a Jacobite relic thought to have been used to give communion before the Battle of Culloden in 1746, while Dom learns the secrets behind hand-blown glass paperweights in Crieff, Perth and Kinross

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams heads for the Peak District, helping a Canadian couple spend up to £475,000 on a new home for them and their dog. Briony also meets some park rangers who keep the landscape in tip-top shape, including octogenarian Margaret who still volunteers on the hills

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don kicks off the horticultural year by cutting back ornamental grasses, planting summer-fruiting raspberries and potting up dahlias. Adam Frost has moved house, and reveals his new garden. And Arit Anderson gets access behind the scenes at the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, where they do vital work in conserving endangered plants from all over the world

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame
programme.desc: The four remaining players from the first heat battle it out for a place in the semi-finals. They are faced with a giant projection screen, and realise an innovative challenge awaits - a memory test set by world number one chess player Magnus Carlsen. Presented by Sue Perkins, with analysis from David Howell and Anthony Mathurin

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive travels to the heart of Sicily to cook lunch in a community kitchen set up by Jamie Olivers former restaurant manager and spends the night in a 700-year-old mansion. He also meets people from around the world who have bought homes in the one Euro house hub of Mussomeli and discovers arguably Italys best street food in the islands capital Palermo

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: Hunger starts to bite for the animals of the Serengeti, who are anticipating the arrival of the annual great migration - and the feast it will bring. Kali the lioness and her sister entrust their cubs to new male babysitter Sefu, with near disastrous results, while Bakari the baboon is shocked when a trip across the river to collect crocodile eggs looks like ending in tragedy for his new female Cheka. A family of wild dogs moves in, led by male Jasari, who is ready to take advantage of the coming feast for his large litter of pups

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team examines finds in Newport, Gwent, including a large teapot, a rare Hungarian vase and an enormous painting of Britannia. Plus, as a testimony to ingenuity, presenter Michael Aspel gets a chance to try out a home-made bomb-clearing device from the Second World War

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and David Fletcher look for valuable items in Dorchester, Dorset, and they come across trench art from the First World War and a 19th-century pistol, while presenter Paul Martin takes a tour of Portland Bill Lighthouse with the keeper

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Steve Backshall, Steve Pemberton, Sam Quek and Rachel Riley test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Steve Backshall, Steve Pemberton, Sam Quek and Rachel Riley test their general knowledge skills in the final round of challenges

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Wales must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee, Phil Wang and guest judge Sally El Hosaini, the Bafta-nominated director of acclaimed film The Swimmers. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Just one positive TB test result could mean quarantine for Lucy and Adams dairy herd and disaster for their move to Scotland. Gayle is banned from the lambing shed to protect her unborn child from an infection transmitted through sheep after birth, which puts extra pressure on Andrew and his dad Terrance. Ally passes on his lambing knowledge to his chidren Bethany and Noah, hoping to increase the numbers of young crofters on Lewis

programme.title: Written by Us
programme.desc: Award-winning concert filmed at Belfasts Grand Opera House celebrating the global impact of songs written by musicians from Northern Ireland. Host Eamonn McCrystal brings together Rachel Tucker, Brian Kennedy, Keith and Kristyn Getty and May McFettridge for an evening of music. From Van Morrison to Snow Patrol and Jimmy Kennedy, the show revisits internationally recognised hits, including Moondance and South of the Border, revealing the Northern Irish writers behind songs that became part of a global soundtrack

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests Nikita Kanda and Linda Plant take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the latest task, and the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day with Patrick OConnell

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Black Waters: The Sea Empress Disaster
programme.desc: Documentary about the impact of the environmental disaster when the Sea Empress tanker ran aground at the entrance to Milford Haven port in 1996. Within hours, thousands of tons of crude oil had poured into the sea, which eventually spread across the coastline of Pembrokeshire, blackening beaches, poisoning wildlife and transforming a thriving tourist region into the centre of an environmental emergency watched by the world

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Tim Renkow welcomes comedians Janine Harouni and Stuart Goldsmith to perform their stand-up routines on Londons Hammersmith Apollo stage

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Death in Paradise

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: This Wasnt the Plan. But Its Where We Are

programme.title: The Bidding Room
