programme.desc: The Lower Zambezii, where some animals struggle while others thrive on the banks of one of Africas greatest rivers

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Wine ditches lessons to make a new friend. Two refugees from a Thai circus get a second chance at living semi-wild on a pre-release island.

programme.title: Rooted
programme.desc: The incredible story of an 800-year-old baobab tree rooted on the savannah of Malilangwe Game Reserve in southern Zimbabwe

programme.title: Finding Your Feet (2017)
programme.desc: A wealthy womans privileged existence is shattered when she discovers her husband is having an affair with her best friend. Seeking solace, she turns to her estranged sister, who lives in a London council estate, and finds a new lease of life in joining her community dance class. Comedy, starring Imelda Staunton, Celia Imrie, Timothy Spall and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: The Wedding Banquet (2025)
programme.desc: A gay man makes a deal with his lesbian friend - a green-card marriage for him, in exchange for in vitro fertilisation treatments for her. Romantic comedy, starring Bowen Yang, Lily Gladstone and Kelly Marie Tran

programme.title: The Taming of the Shrew (1967)
programme.desc: A young womans father forbids her to marry until her older sister is wed first - a tall order given the elder siblings fiery temper and sharp tongue. When a fortune-hunting rogue arrives in town, three men keen to marry the younger sister persuade him to woo the older. Franco Zeffirellis adaptation of William Shakespeares comedy, starring Elizabeth Taylor and Richard Burton

programme.title: Forces of Nature (1999)
programme.desc: A harassed publisher faces several unexpected set-backs on the way to his wedding - including a chance encounter with a vivacious woman, who only adds to his confusion as he begins to feel stirrings of love for her. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Ben Affleck, Maura Tierney, Steve Zahn, Blythe Danner and Ronny Cox

programme.title: Mamma Mia! (2008)
programme.desc: A former member of a singing trio, now running a hotel on a Greek island, is reunited with her old bandmates for her daughters wedding - but is surprised when three of her old flames also show up unexpectedly. The three men have been invited by the bride, who wants to find out which one of them is her father. Musical comedy, with a soundtrack of Abba songs, starring Meryl Streep, Pierce Brosnan, Julie Walters, Colin Firth, Stellan Skarsgard and Amanda Seyfried

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: Its five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. Along the way, she learns more about her mothers past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: The Holiday (2006)
programme.desc: Two single women living on opposite sides of the Atlantic agree to exchange houses over the Christmas holidays. In their new surroundings, both women unexpectedly encounter prospective partners, but as the house swap draws to a close, difficult decisions have to be made about their blossoming relationships. Romantic comedy, starring Cameron Diaz, Kate Winslet, Jack Black, Jude Law and Eli Wallach

programme.title: Miss Congeniality (2000)
programme.desc: A feminist FBI agent is sent undercover as a contestant in the Miss United States pageant when the event becomes the target of a terrorist bomb threat. As she is not exactly catwalk material, a veteran British coach is brought on board to oversee her transformation into a glamorous beauty queen. Comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Michael Caine, Benjamin Bratt, Candice Bergen and William Shatner

programme.title: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days (2003)
programme.desc: A female journalist sets out to demonstrate how women manage to put off prospective partners, and spies the perfect man on whom to test her theories. However, the experiment takes an unexpected turn thanks to her guinea pig, who has a hidden agenda of his own. Romantic comedy, starring Kate Hudson, Matthew McConaughey, Adam Goldberg and Michael Michele

programme.title: The 40 Year-Old Virgin (2005)
programme.desc: A middle-aged shop worker reveals he has never been intimate with a woman, which prompts his shocked colleagues to set him up with a series of potentially passionate dates. When he falls for a single mother, his friends think he is right on course for a lusty liaison, but his new partner is not ready for a physical relationship. Judd Apatows comedy, starring Steve Carell, Catherine Keener and Paul Rudd

programme.title: Love Actually (2003)
programme.desc: Ten interlinked stories of people searching for and finding love. While the newly elected, bachelor British prime minister is smitten by a junior staff member at 10 Downing Street, a heartbroken writer heads for France to get over a failed relationship, and a faded rock star aims to reinvent himself in time for Christmas. Richard Curtiss romantic comedy, with Hugh Grant, Martine McCutcheon, Emma Thompson, Alan Rickman, Colin Firth, Bill Nighy and Liam Neeson

programme.title: Singin in the Rain (1952)
programme.desc: A silent-movie star tries to win the heart of an aspiring actress as the introduction of sound changes the film business for ever. However, his frequent on-screen love interest does not take kindly to this real-life romance. Musical comedy, starring Gene Kelly, Debbie Reynolds, Donald OConnor, Jean Hagen and Millard Mitchell. Songs include Make em Laugh, You Are My Lucky Star and the title number

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: Its five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. Along the way, she learns more about her mothers past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: Forces of Nature (1999)
programme.desc: A harassed publisher faces several unexpected set-backs on the way to his wedding - including a chance encounter with a vivacious woman, who only adds to his confusion as he begins to feel stirrings of love for her. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Ben Affleck, Maura Tierney, Steve Zahn, Blythe Danner and Ronny Cox

programme.title: Hope Springs (2012)
programme.desc: A middle-aged married woman feels her long-standing relationship with her unadventurous husband is becoming stale. When she hears of a marriage counsellor offering therapy sessions in his remote retreat, she persuades her reluctant spouse to join her for a week with the doctor rediscovering why they fell in love in the first place. Romantic comedy, with Meryl Streep, Tommy Lee Jones and Steve Carell

programme.title: The First Wives Club (1996)
programme.desc: Three former college friends reunite at the funeral of a mutual acquaintance, who killed herself after her husband left her for a younger woman. Finding themselves in a similar situation, the trio decide to take action and hit their former partners where it hurts most - their finances. Comedy, starring Goldie Hawn, Bette Midler, Diane Keaton, Maggie Smith, Dan Hedaya, Bronson Pinchot and Elizabeth Berkley

programme.title: Just Go with It (2011)
programme.desc: A plastic surgeon pretends to be unhappily married to seduce women. When his girlfriend finds his prop wedding ring, he claims he is getting a divorce and has to persuade his assistant to pose as his soon-to-be ex-wife, but is forced to come up with yet more lies to maintain the illusion. Romantic comedy, starring Adam Sandler, Jennifer Aniston and Nicole Kidman

programme.title: Love Actually (2003)
programme.desc: Ten interlinked stories of people searching for and finding love. While the newly elected, bachelor British prime minister is smitten by a junior staff member at 10 Downing Street, a heartbroken writer heads for France to get over a failed relationship, and a faded rock star aims to reinvent himself in time for Christmas. Richard Curtiss romantic comedy, with Hugh Grant, Martine McCutcheon, Emma Thompson, Alan Rickman, Colin Firth, Bill Nighy and Liam Neeson

programme.title: The 40 Year-Old Virgin (2005)
programme.desc: A middle-aged shop worker reveals he has never been intimate with a woman, which prompts his shocked colleagues to set him up with a series of potentially passionate dates. When he falls for a single mother, his friends think he is right on course for a lusty liaison, but his new partner is not ready for a physical relationship. Judd Apatows comedy, starring Steve Carell, Catherine Keener and Paul Rudd

programme.title: The Wedding Singer (1998)
programme.desc: A failed rock star carves out a career as a popular wedding singer, but his hopes for a bright future are dashed when his fiancee jilts him at the altar and he tries to shut himself off from the outside world. At one of his functions, a waitress catches his eye - but unfortunately for him, she is already engaged to someone else. Romantic comedy set in the 1980s, starring Adam Sandler and Drew Barrymore

programme.title: Sleepless in Seattle (1993)
programme.desc: A widowed father is coping badly with his wifes death, but his son is convinced that what he really needs is some new romance in his life. He arranges for his dad to go on a radio phone-in show, and his touching story moves a reporter to respond, even though she is already engaged. She returns to her fiance, but the boy, certain she is the woman for his father, plots to bring them together. Romantic comedy, with Tom Hanks and Meg Ryan

programme.title: Bridget Joness Diary (2001)
programme.desc: A single woman begins a new year with resolutions to find love, quit her bad habits and take a step up the career ladder. Her plans crumble as she tries to deal with her mothers infidelity, get over a passionate fling with her boss and come to terms with her feelings for an uptight lawyer. Romantic comedy based on Helen Fieldings novel, starring Renee Zellweger, Hugh Grant and Colin Firth

programme.title: Bridget Jones: The Edge of Reason (2004)
programme.desc: The disaster-prone journalist seems to have finally found the perfect boyfriend. However, when the couple start to squabble over their personal habits, she decides to decamp for a while to Thailand and write a travelogue. Unfortunately, she has reckoned without the reappearance of a duplicitous old flame - and a spell behind bars in a Thai prison. Romantic comedy sequel, with Renee Zellweger, Colin Firth, Hugh Grant, Jim Broadbent and Gemma Jones

programme.title: Bridget Joness Baby (2016)
programme.desc: Bridgets focus on single life and her career is interrupted when she finds herself pregnant, but with one hitch - she can only be 50 per cent sure of the identity of her babys father after a pair of one-night stands. Is the dad old flame Mark Darcy or US dating guru Jack Qwant? Comedy sequel, starring Renee Zellweger, Colin Firth, Patrick Dempsey, Emma Thompson and Sally Phillips

programme.title: Just Go with It (2011)
programme.desc: A plastic surgeon pretends to be unhappily married to seduce women. When his girlfriend finds his prop wedding ring, he claims he is getting a divorce and has to persuade his assistant to pose as his soon-to-be ex-wife, but is forced to come up with yet more lies to maintain the illusion. Romantic comedy, starring Adam Sandler, Jennifer Aniston and Nicole Kidman

programme.title: Sometimes I Think About Dying (2023)
programme.desc: Fran is an office worker who lives in a world of her own on the dreary Oregon coast. At home she leads a solitary, silent existence, and dreams that shes lying dead on a forest floor. Fran eventually struggles to make a connection with a friendly co-worker who takes her on a date. Comedy drama, starring Daisy Ridley, Dave Merheje and Parvesh Cheena

programme.title: Book of Love (2021)
programme.desc: Young, uptight and unsuccessful English writer Henrys latest novel sells poorly. But when the book suddenly becomes a surprise hit in Mexico, his publicist insists he travel there on a promotional tour. Upon arrival, a confused Henry discovers the reason behind the popularity - Mexican translator Maria has rewritten the dull book as a steamy erotic story. A furious Henry and reluctant Maria must now travel together on a book tour across Mexico. As tempers flair and sparks fly, the two begin to find love. Romantic comedy, starring Sam Claflin and Veronica Echegui

programme.title: Miss Congeniality (2000)
programme.desc: A feminist FBI agent is sent undercover as a contestant in the Miss United States pageant when the event becomes the target of a terrorist bomb threat. As she is not exactly catwalk material, a veteran British coach is brought on board to oversee her transformation into a glamorous beauty queen. Comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Michael Caine, Benjamin Bratt, Candice Bergen and William Shatner

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous (2005)
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie Hart is taken off active duty and made the face of the bureaus publicity campaign, but would rather catch crooks than be the focus of media attention - so when her friends are kidnapped, she disobeys orders to run her own undercover investigation with the help of a short-tempered colleague. Comedy sequel, starring Sandra Bullock, Regina King, William Shatner and Heather Burns

programme.title: Young Sheldon

programme.title: Young Sheldon

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Comic-book store owner Stuart asks Amy out on a date, forcing a jealous Sheldon to consider the future of his relationship with her. Meanwhile, the scientists become obsessed with an unusual new card game, and Penny organises a round of Twister. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Mayim Bialik

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard confronts the man who tormented him in high school, while Penny realises she did her fair share of bullying in the past and calls the people she targeted to apologise. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is uninterested in Amys career, so Penny, Raj and Leonard try to make him understand that sometimes it benefits a relationship to pretend to care about a partners achievements. Howard notices Bernadette is uncomfortable around children when she helps him perform a magic show at his nephews birthday party

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard asks Penny out on a date, and they agree to give their relationship another chance. They soon remember why it did not work out in the first place, but end up sleeping together anyway. The 100th episode of the comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny continue testing their rekindled relationship using an innovative system to report discrepancies. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After a sleepless night, Leonard breaks his room-mate agreement with Sheldon. Howard makes preparations to go on a flight to the International Space Station and enlists Rajs help to ensure he gets a cool astronaut nickname

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon uses his holidays to help Amy in her biology lab, but moans about the menial jobs he is given - until she asks him to dissect a brain. Penny grows curious about Leonards attitude to marriage when Bernadette asks Howard to sign a pre-nuptial agreement

programme.title: Mike and Molly
programme.desc: Mike plans a special Valentines Day surprise for Molly in the form of a custom-made cake, but is dismayed when he discovers the handsome baker he ordered the gift from is actually her ex-boyfriend. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Mike and Molly
programme.sub-title: Joyce and Vince and Peaches and Herb

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: Spring sees the daffodil harvest, accompanied by a rush to get the flowers off to the train station so they can be delivered to towns and cities across the country within hours of being picked. programme.title: Edwardian Farm

She also explores one of the growing fashions of the era by holding a seance, while once back on dry land, Alex and Peter drive their cattle along a dangerous route to find a new pasture

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Mark Evans meets more people who aspire to live in their ultimate waterside dream home. Simon and Mary Sparrow live on a barge in Beccles, Suffolk, with their son Nate and their two cats. The Sparrows have been running a boatyard for nearly 10 years, specialising in refurbishing and renting out floating homes to holidaymakers. Now theyre designing and building a new, one-of-a-kind floating home for themselves - a futuristic-looking floating pod, drawing on a budget of £70,000

programme.desc: Reclaimed bricks are used during a masterclass in old-English brickwork from the ground up

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets Revealed
programme.desc: Consumer show lifting the lid on secrets the major food retailers would rather keep to themselves

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn set sail in an Edwardian trawler for a fishing trip, but as women were considered bad luck at sea, Ruth Goodman is left ashore to forage on the beach, prepare potted shrimp and build a smoke-house to cure mackerel. She also explores one of the growing fashions of the era by holding a seance, while once back on dry land, Alex and Peter drive their cattle along a dangerous route to find a new pasture

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: Marcus decides to bring in some sheep in order to produce his favourite meat - lamb. A trip to his native Lancashire introduces him to the amazing Lonk sheep, famed for their delicious meat. Back at the smallholding, while waiting for his new sheep to arrive, he learns about growing ones own spices from chilli enthusiast Mothin and batches up some tomato sauce to share at the local food bank. Once his sheep have arrived, he cooks up a meaty feast for the people who have helped him along the way

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team help Craig and Becky from Keynsham turn their awkward new build garden into a relaxing, contemporary, zen garden for them and their three boys on a budget of £5,000

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Mark Evans meets more people who aspire to live in their ultimate waterside dream home. Simon and Mary Sparrow live on a barge in Beccles, Suffolk, with their son Nate and their two cats. The Sparrows have been running a boatyard for nearly 10 years, specialising in refurbishing and renting out floating homes to holidaymakers. Now theyre designing and building a new, one-of-a-kind floating home for themselves - a futuristic-looking floating pod, drawing on a budget of £70,000

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets Revealed
programme.desc: Consumer show lifting the lid on secrets the major food retailers would rather keep to themselves

programme.title: Musketeer
programme.desc: An old musketeer meets a youth and two scoundrels on the road to Paris. Short drama, starring Kevin McNally, Andrew Lewis and Charlie Allen

programme.title: Some Like It Sexy (1969)
programme.desc: A young man in swinging London has a series of sexual misadventures with women from all walks of life, including a hippie, a high society ladys daughter, and a salvation army officer. Erotic comedy, starring Christopher Matthews and Yolande Turner

programme.title: The Blood on Satans Claw (1971)
programme.desc: Strange remains are discovered in a 17th-century farming village, and have a disturbing effect on the townsfolk. They form a sinister cult, performing strange and violent rituals to bring their demonic master back to life. However, a pious judge is prepared to use brutal methods of his own to root out the evil. Horror, starring Patrick Wymark, Linda Hayden and Simon Williams

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: Empire Day approaches and a paddle steamer docks at the Morwellham Quay for the first time in 80 years. Such vessels were frequent visitors when Edwardian tourists first began to visit the Tamar Valley, and Alex Langlands, Peter Ginn and Ruth Goodman put on a party for the passengers, but have to learn how to make clotted cream and the Devonshire version of a scone. Plus, the trio harvest their strawberries

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Alan and Val McCoy put their life savings on the line to make their dream of living on water come true, entrusting a boat builder to make them their dream narrow boat floating home. The couple have been together for 11 years and now that theyre retired, theyve decided that owning a narrow boat could be a unique way to be near their families - all of whom live along the canal system across the Midlands and north-west England

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: Dave Wellman prepares to repair and replaster a bedroom ceiling in a family home

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: Dave is in Worcestershire, widening access to a couples property by building a concrete platform and new gateway

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden

programme.title: The Deadly Females (1976)

programme.title: Skyfire (2019)
programme.desc: Tianhuo Island is a remote paradise where businessman Jack Harris is about to open a new holiday resort. With its stunning views and the chance to tour the dormant volcano, visitors flock there to experience the sights. But when the mountain decides to wake up, it creates terror in its wake. Director Simon Wests disaster thriller, starring Jason Isaacs, Alice Rietveld and Xueqi Wang. In Mandarin and English with subtitles

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: Acting against the wishes of his boss, Patrick agrees to prove a convicted murderers innocence in exchange for vital information about Red John, the man who killed his wife and daughter. programme.title: My Life Is Murder

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mrs McCarthys friend is the latest villager to die in her sleep, and Father Brown soon senses something sinister is afoot. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The leader of a spiritual retreat is found strangled en route to the contemplation garden, giving Jack plenty to think about as he tries to figure out which member of the group was responsible. The problem is, they were all meditating at the time of the murder, and are able to provide one another with alibis, so how could any of them be the killer? To add to the DIs problems, he is struggling to come up with a speech to honour Commissioner Patterson at an award ceremony. Caribbean whodunit, starring Ardal OHanlon

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team become embroiled in the mysterious world of faith healing when a blind woman dies while being treated by renowned healer Steadman King. When it turns out the victim was poisoned, all eyes turn to Steadman and his cup of holy water. But how could the prime suspect have carried out the deed in front of an audience without anyone noticing? Meanwhile, JP is put through his paces when Pico the police dog joins the team on a week-long trial, and Florence tries to recreate some of Jacks home comforts

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: Alexas past catches up to her as she returns to her exclusive, private high school to investigate the mysterious death of her much beloved former teacher. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: The Mentalist

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: Murder on the Day of the Dead
programme.desc: It seems somebody has taken the islands Day of the Dead celebrations a little too literally when a man receives a voicemail from his wife saying she is being attacked. When she turns up dead, suspicion falls upon her friends and acquaintances at the nearby yacht club - her husband Finn, club secretary Charlotte and chairman Hugh. However, Jacks curiosity is piqued by a yellow butterfly found on the victims clothing and he calls upon Commissioner Selwyns knowledge of such matters. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring John Sessions and Pippa Haywood

programme.desc: Olympic long jump champion Greg Rutherford, broadcaster Kirsty Young, actress-writer Joanna Scanlan and comedian Henning Wehn join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the panel show, trying to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon hosts

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. programme.desc: The seafood chef continues to explore the Far Easts most diverse food cultures with a visit to Sri Lanka, where he discovers a wealth of hot and spicy vegetable curries. programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers

programme.desc: Richard Hammond tests Volkswagens Touareg off-roader, before driving a racing version of the car against a group of snowmobile riders in Sweden. programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Queens of Mystery
programme.desc: Part one of two. The stage adaptation of Janes book endures numerous problems before opening night, including electrical problems, feuding cast members and a notorious arts critic writing a potentially bad review. Meanwhile, Mattie searches for new clues about her missing mother, but an onstage murder brings her to the theatre

programme.title: Blue Lights
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the shooting, the recruits are determined the McIntyres will pay for what they have done. Stevie goes to arrest the MI5 operatives - but Joseph orders Jonty to let them go free. Tina demands that James tells Mo his dark secret, and Grace accepts that she might have to break her promise to Angela

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the 10-year-old murder of a librarian who also belonged to a witches coven, and tries to track down the other members of the group. Halford has visions of his dead wife, and becomes convinced he has been cursed, while Lane starts to suspect the killing may have been part of a human sacrifice. With guest star Stephanie Beacham

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lus puzzle-solving skills are put to the test when they investigate a mysterious car accident which killed a young mother

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley returns to his ancestral home to celebrate his engagement, but 20 years of anger over his mothers indiscretion with a doctor makes the occasion unpleasant. A murder in a nearby village provides him with a temporary distraction - until mounting evidence appears to suggest his brother is implicated. Mystery drama, starring Nathaniel Parker

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: An activity weekend lands Dorien in hospital - where she is mortified that the private doctors might think she fraternises with the likes of Sharon and Tracey. Veteran TV weatherman Ian McCaskill appears as himself

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: The Queen of Poisons: Part Two
programme.desc: As the suspects secrets stack up, Judith, Becks and Suzie still cant find anyone with a motive to hurt the Mayor. Guest starring Peter Davison

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey discovers more than she bargained for while sorting out jumble for a car-boot sale after chancing upon an old photo album. Linda Robson stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Burnside is forced to arrest his colleague Carver on suspicion of murder

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Melody helps a couple troubled by the husbands alcoholism and Eva is led to the Mill on her search for a hit-and-run driver. Ruth finds a training day with Vivien difficult and Nick has an awkward exchange with Ronnie. Medical drama, starring Elizabeth Bower and Angela Lonsdale

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A wife drags her husband to the surgery to undergo tests for impotency, but he gets the surprise of his life to see his mistress there, seeking advice about a problem of her own. Fortunately, Nick is on hand to help the distraught man unravel the situation. Michael McKell and Henry Watson star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Will questions Zubins handling of Toms dismissal during an operation, accusing him of back-stabbing, and Donna is surprised by how seriously Lisa takes her new responsibilities. On the anniversary of baby Amandas death, Mubbs tells Chrissie the identity of the father, while Diane reveals to Owen that she has been raped in the past

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: The house is rocked by controversy when the publication of a scandalous novel exposes Blanches unconventional lifestyle, bringing the doctor face to face with the author of the book and her former lover Lady Portia Alresford. But when the passion between the pair reignites, the doctor becomes conflicted. Meanwhile, Persie reveals a shocking secret to Hallam, and Beryls workload prompts her to take a stand - until a familiar old face appears to offer a few wise words. Emilia Fox guest stars, with Alex Kingston, Jean Marsh, Ed Stoppard and Claire Foy

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.sub-title: A Faraway Country About Which We Know Nothing
programme.desc: Drama and intrigue with the aristocrats and serving staff of 165 Eaton Place as it opens its doors once more. September 1938, and as dark clouds gather over Europe, Hallams preoccupation with Nazi Germany leads him into dangerous waters overseas. Back home, Lady Agnes is frail following the birth of her second child, and finds great support in the forthright Aunt Blanche, who has made herself comfortable at Eaton Place following the funeral of her sister Lady Holland. In the servants quarters, Rose is taken ill with TB, so the staff welcome spirited new maid Beryl, who catches Harrys eye, while events upstairs end in a startling revelation about Mr Pritchard. Keeley Hawes, Ed Stoppard, Alex Kingston and Adrian Scarborough star

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.sub-title: The Love That Pays the Price
programme.desc: Lady Agnes distracts herself from her bad news by throwing a dinner party, where she catches the eye of charismatic American millionaire Casper Landry - and it is clear the attraction is mutual. But the festivities are interrupted by news of the Nazis going on the rampage in Germany, leaving many Jews dead and Lady Persie needing a safe passage home, forcing the family to pull together and do its best to help. Meanwhile, Mrs Thackeray has one too many arguments with Mr Pritchard, prompting her to make a momentous decision that throws the running of the house into chaos

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: The house is rocked by controversy when the publication of a scandalous novel exposes Blanches unconventional lifestyle, bringing the doctor face to face with the author of the book and her former lover Lady Portia Alresford. But when the passion between the pair reignites, the doctor becomes conflicted. Meanwhile, Persie reveals a shocking secret to Hallam, and Beryls workload prompts her to take a stand - until a familiar old face appears to offer a few wise words. Emilia Fox guest stars, with Alex Kingston, Jean Marsh, Ed Stoppard and Claire Foy

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team are called in to save the survivors of a fatal crash and theres a bomb alert after Afghan asylum seekers are moved into a seaside hotel

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of the Lancashire village Sunderland Point, which is cut off twice a day by the tide and has become a haven for wildlife. He reveals it was once a busy port that was instrumental in the slave trade, and at the forefront of the luxury goods business that transformed 18th-century Britain.

programme.desc: The Doctor completes his escape from the Daleks and is reunited with Jo before taking shelter in the Plain of Stones. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jon Pertwee

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: Planet of the Daleks - Part Five
programme.desc: Jo learns of the Dalek plot to release a bacteria that will kill all unimmunised life, leading to a two-pronged attack on their base

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: Planet of the Daleks - Part Six
programme.desc: The Doctor and his cohorts strive to thwart the Daleks evil scheme just as more of their number arrive, including the Dalek Supreme

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of six. Classic episode of the sci-fi series, starring Jon Pertwee. The Doctor is called in to investigate when a chemical plant in Wales is rocked by a series of mysterious deaths, with the bodies of the victims inexplicably turned green

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of six. Jo is trapped at the bottom of the mine, where she discovers another worker affected by the strange green infection. The Doctor discovers the lift has been sabotaged, and the local people mount a protest against the chemical plant

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and Jo discover the source of the infection - an underground lake of toxic waste inhabited by mutated maggots. However, a cave-in leaves them trapped in the mine with the deadly creatures. Meanwhile, the Brigadier comes under pressure from the Government. Jon Pertwee stars

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim tries to improve employment figures by moving military units around the country. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim tries to censor part of a publication by the former prime minister that portrays him in a bad light. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim tries to turn the tables on French officials over the Channel Tunnel borders. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim gets wind of a major financial scandal in the City that is bound to have damaging repercussions for the government. With the party conference looming, he decides its time for him to take swift and firm action - but Sir Humphrey has other ideas. Political comedy first broadcast in 1987, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim feels local government isnt really representative, so is eager to hear more about proposals to reform the system. Sir Humphrey fears this would lead to the civil service losing its power and turns to an unlikely ally to help persuade the prime minister to think again. Political comedy first broadcast in 1988, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of six. Classic episode of the sci-fi series, starring Jon Pertwee. The Doctor is called in to investigate when a chemical plant in Wales is rocked by a series of mysterious deaths, with the bodies of the victims inexplicably turned green

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of six. Jo is trapped at the bottom of the mine, where she discovers another worker affected by the strange green infection. The Doctor discovers the lift has been sabotaged, and the local people mount a protest against the chemical plant

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and Jo discover the source of the infection - an underground lake of toxic waste inhabited by mutated maggots. However, a cave-in leaves them trapped in the mine with the deadly creatures. Meanwhile, the Brigadier comes under pressure from the Government. Jon Pertwee stars

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: Planet of the Daleks - Part Four
programme.desc: The Doctor completes his escape from the Daleks and is reunited with Jo before taking shelter in the Plain of Stones. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jon Pertwee

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim tries to improve employment figures by moving military units around the country. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim tries to censor part of a publication by the former prime minister that portrays him in a bad light. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim tries to turn the tables on French officials over the Channel Tunnel borders. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim gets wind of a major financial scandal in the City that is bound to have damaging repercussions for the government. With the party conference looming, he decides its time for him to take swift and firm action - but Sir Humphrey has other ideas. Political comedy first broadcast in 1987, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim feels local government isnt really representative, so is eager to hear more about proposals to reform the system. Sir Humphrey fears this would lead to the civil service losing its power and turns to an unlikely ally to help persuade the prime minister to think again. Political comedy first broadcast in 1988, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Spooner reveals unexpected musical abilities - and the rest of the staff, astonished to discover he has any talent at all, insist on cashing in on his chart success. Mike Berry, John Inman, Frank Thornton, Wendy Richard and Mollie Sugden star in the final episode of the long-running shopfloor comedy, which features a guest appearance by former Crimewatch presenter Nick Ross

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg comes off his bike after cycling downhill and sustains a fractured foot. Truly believes that the accident would never have happened if the vehicle had been fitted with studded tyres - and immediately sets about organising a practical demonstration of his theory

programme.title: Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster meets Bronx businessman Alex Henriquez, who was convicted for three murders in 1992. The victims had two things in common - they were all asphyxiated before being dumped by the highway and all three could be placed with Alex Henriquez shortly before their deaths. For 30 years the killer has protested his innocence, claiming the police set him up. Piers Morgan tries to uncover the truth

programme.title: Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.sub-title: Confessions of a Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster comes face to face with Bernard Giles, who admits to picking up five women hitch-hiking through his neighbourhood in late 1973 and killing them all in cold blood. Fortunate to be spared the death penalty, Giles will never be released from prison. Now Piers tries to get inside his mind, to explore his psychological make-up and understand what compelled him to commit these brutal crimes

programme.title: Brit Cops Down Under
programme.sub-title: Its Not All Beaches and Blue Skies
programme.desc: Anna hits the first milestone in Australia without the support of family: Isabelles 8th birthday. Jay settles into work, but life at home is not so smooth

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Experts Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel Britain to find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners, turning items into hard cash. A butter churn, a slot machine and a set of print trays are among the finds in this edition

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, helps a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a small house. Sandras daughter Crystal Chambers passed away in 2015, so she and Crystals 19-year-old brother Ziggy are doing their best to raise youngsters Deago and Ameira amid cramped conditions

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nick Knowles and the team undertake their biggest and longest build yet, constructing two buildings for the Grenfell community over several months to create spaces worth around £2million. With the aid of large construction companies, generous suppliers, volunteers and the Duke of Cambridge - who visits the site - the team starts by building a new home for the Dale Youth Boxing Club. The club started the careers of James DeGale, who won an Olympic gold medal in Beijing 10 years ago, and George Groves, current WBA Super Middleweight champion, as well as producing many national champions

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats nine-month-old cockapoo Betsy, whose owners Sophie and Dan are concerned about a dragging front paw caused by nerve damage, to see if there are surgical options. But when Betsy develops gangrene, she has to have her paw amputated, and in order to salvage the rest of her limb, Noel must replace her amputated foot with a skeletally-anchored implant called a PerFiTS (Percutaneous Fixation to the Skeleton)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary featuring one of the most emotional and heart-rending stories ever filmed - that of Noel Fitzpatricks own dog, Keira. In September 2020, Noels life was turned upside down when the 13-year-old border terrier was hit by a car right outside the practice. Having sustained a ruptured abdominal lining and urinary bladder, a crushed sacrum and pelvis and a dislocated hip, Noels colleagues worked tirelessly to repair extensive internal damage. Suddenly, the surgeon renowned for finding solutions for even the most challenging injuries, had to steel himself for the most important surgery of his life

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS team transform the home of a police officer who was a victim of the Westminster Bridge terrorist attack in March 2017, suffering horrific injuries which left him wheelchair bound. Months after the attack took place, Kris still resides in hospital as his north London home is inaccessible for him, so the team and hundreds of volunteers work to get Kris living back at home to his family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, helps a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a small house. Sandras daughter Crystal Chambers passed away in 2015, so she and Crystals 19-year-old brother Ziggy are doing their best to raise youngsters Deago and Ameira amid cramped conditions

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance

programme.title: How to Catch a Serial Killer
programme.desc: Former detective Steven Fulcher breaks down the risky investigation that caught infamous murderer Christopher Halliwell - and ended his policing career

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: True-crime series hosted by Below Deck fan favourite Captain Lee Rosbach, who uses his nautical expertise to explore chilling murders that have happened on lakes, rivers and seas. When a Bahamas-bound boat is found abandoned 140 miles off course, investigators wade through blood evidence and reports of a possible pirate attack as they look for answers

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Journalist and broadcaster Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of Americas most notorious female killers in a quest to discover what drives women to kill. He travels to Gatesville, Texas, to visit Erin Caffey, who masterminded the murder of her entire family at the age of 16, and will remain behind bars until she is almost 60. Piers finds out what made a typical teenager conspire with her boyfriend to kill her mum, dad, and two younger brothers, before meeting the only survivor of the tragedy, Erins father Terry

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Journalist and TV personality Piers Morgan continues his travels across the USA, this time meeting two very different female murderers, both of whom killed someone they loved. First, he heads to Floridas largest female prison to visit Amanda Hutto, who is serving a life sentence for murdering her daughter. She was accused of deliberately dunking her seven-year-old child as a punishment for being naughty, with her young son testifying against her at the murder trial. Next, he journeys to Texas, where he encounters Rhonda Glover, a former beauty queen and rodeo star who gunned down her lover in cold blood after their relationship spiralled into drug-fuelled obsession and paranoia

programme.title: Brit Cops Down Under
programme.desc: The Khan family have embraced life in Perth so fully that theyve decided to make it permanent by purchasing land, while Anna is struggling with the shift hours

programme.sub-title: Confessions of a Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster comes face to face with Bernard Giles, who admits to picking up five women hitch-hiking through his neighbourhood in late 1973 and killing them all in cold blood. Fortunate to be spared the death penalty, Giles will never be released from prison. Now Piers tries to get inside his mind, to explore his psychological make-up and understand what compelled him to commit these brutal crimes

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Experts Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel Britain to find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners, turning items into hard cash. A butter churn, a slot machine and a set of print trays are among the finds in this edition

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, helps a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a small house. Sandras daughter Crystal Chambers passed away in 2015, so she and Crystals 19-year-old brother Ziggy are doing their best to raise youngsters Deago and Ameira amid cramped conditions

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nick Knowles and the team undertake their biggest and longest build yet, constructing two buildings for the Grenfell community over several months to create spaces worth around £2million. With the aid of large construction companies, generous suppliers, volunteers and the Duke of Cambridge - who visits the site - the team starts by building a new home for the Dale Youth Boxing Club. The club started the careers of James DeGale, who won an Olympic gold medal in Beijing 10 years ago, and George Groves, current WBA Super Middleweight champion, as well as producing many national champions

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats nine-month-old cockapoo Betsy, whose owners Sophie and Dan are concerned about a dragging front paw caused by nerve damage, to see if there are surgical options. But when Betsy develops gangrene, she has to have her paw amputated, and in order to salvage the rest of her limb, Noel must replace her amputated foot with a skeletally-anchored implant called a PerFiTS (Percutaneous Fixation to the Skeleton)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary featuring one of the most emotional and heart-rending stories ever filmed - that of Noel Fitzpatricks own dog, Keira. In September 2020, Noels life was turned upside down when the 13-year-old border terrier was hit by a car right outside the practice. Having sustained a ruptured abdominal lining and urinary bladder, a crushed sacrum and pelvis and a dislocated hip, Noels colleagues worked tirelessly to repair extensive internal damage. Suddenly, the surgeon renowned for finding solutions for even the most challenging injuries, had to steel himself for the most important surgery of his life

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS team transform the home of a police officer who was a victim of the Westminster Bridge terrorist attack in March 2017, suffering horrific injuries which left him wheelchair bound. Months after the attack took place, Kris still resides in hospital as his north London home is inaccessible for him, so the team and hundreds of volunteers work to get Kris living back at home to his family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, helps a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a small house. Sandras daughter Crystal Chambers passed away in 2015, so she and Crystals 19-year-old brother Ziggy are doing their best to raise youngsters Deago and Ameira amid cramped conditions

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance

programme.title: How to Catch a Serial Killer
programme.desc: Former detective Steven Fulcher breaks down the risky investigation that caught infamous murderer Christopher Halliwell - and ended his policing career

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: True-crime series hosted by Below Deck fan favourite Captain Lee Rosbach, who uses his nautical expertise to explore chilling murders that have happened on lakes, rivers and seas. When a Bahamas-bound boat is found abandoned 140 miles off course, investigators wade through blood evidence and reports of a possible pirate attack as they look for answers

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Journalist and broadcaster Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of Americas most notorious female killers in a quest to discover what drives women to kill. He travels to Gatesville, Texas, to visit Erin Caffey, who masterminded the murder of her entire family at the age of 16, and will remain behind bars until she is almost 60. Piers finds out what made a typical teenager conspire with her boyfriend to kill her mum, dad, and two younger brothers, before meeting the only survivor of the tragedy, Erins father Terry

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Journalist and TV personality Piers Morgan continues his travels across the USA, this time meeting two very different female murderers, both of whom killed someone they loved. First, he heads to Floridas largest female prison to visit Amanda Hutto, who is serving a life sentence for murdering her daughter. She was accused of deliberately dunking her seven-year-old child as a punishment for being naughty, with her young son testifying against her at the murder trial. Next, he journeys to Texas, where he encounters Rhonda Glover, a former beauty queen and rodeo star who gunned down her lover in cold blood after their relationship spiralled into drug-fuelled obsession and paranoia

programme.title: Brit Cops Down Under
programme.desc: The Khan family have embraced life in Perth so fully that theyve decided to make it permanent by purchasing land, while Anna is struggling with the shift hours

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of admiring Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his immaculate 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul and Jack head to Staffordshire to pick up five vehicles from collector Robs colossal barn, including a Mini and a Morris Minor

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves in Lincolnshire picking up a 1935 Riley Falcon - a good-looking saloon car, with a pre-select gearbox

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: An examination of the dark history behind Britains fascination with murder, beginning with historian and curator Lucy Worsley exploring how notorious killings were transformed into popular entertainment in the first half of the 19th century

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Ben Robinson visits Laxey on the Isle of Man to discover what drew Victorians to the tourist destination. It wasnt the beaches and the mountains that attracted Victorian tourists, but an engineering marvel - the biggest waterwheel in the world

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Ben Robinson discovers more about the quiet village of Ravenglass in the Lake Districts National Park, which played a strategic role in the Roman empire. With the help of the Pennington family, whose ancestral home is Muncaster Castle, Ben is shown their latest Roman find, a unique stone slab with a Latin inscription thats been attributed to a Roman naval unit, Aelia Classica

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Yorkshire coastal village of Flamborough and discovers its historic chalk buildings. Local historian and archaeologist Andrew Jones shows Ben around Flamborough Tower. Constructed in 1674 and standing 87 feet tall, the tower was built with the purpose of improving ship to shore navigation but would ultimately be used by local law enforcers in a bid to prevent smuggling

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Norfolk village of Cley next the Sea and finds that due to Dutch settlers in the Elizabethan era, its not as next the sea as it once was. Ben talks to historian Onyeka Nubia, who reveals that the village was part of a cosmopolitan community that introduced Britain to the canary and even the beer people drink today

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek brings a small army of military hardware to Thornton le Dale including a Studebaker Weasel tracked vehicle and a WWII-era Willys Jeep

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul collects a cherished 1924 Rover 9 thats been in the same family since the 1950s and only had one previous owner before that

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The story of how car designer Ferdinand Porsche became one of the most influential engineers in all Nazi Germany

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: By April 1945 most of Germany was occupied by invading Allied forces, with the two-million strong Soviet army just a mile from Hitlers hiding place In Berlin. With the Nazi regime disintegrating, most of Hitlers deputies were busy making plans to try to survive the end of the war and only his most die-hard loyalists stay by his side

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from MediaCityUK on the former Manchester docks, and investigates the history of the citys ship canal, which links it to the Irish Sea. Items brought in to be valued include an early animation machine, a statue of Buddha, a magicians box of tricks, and the heaviest treasure ever featured on the show - a 10-metre Canadian totem pole which has to be craned in

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer pier in Norfolk, where items brought in to be valued include a strange machine labelled Certain Death, the Duke of Wellingtons night cap, a note related to Oliver Cromwell, and letters from both Queen Victoria and John Lennon. Plus, Fiona is challenged to guess the values of three rare pieces of antique jewellery

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.sub-title: Jowett Javelin and Escort Barn Find
programme.desc: Derek dives into the lost Jowett marque, as a Bradford-built 1952 Javelin finds an unexpected home. Paul and Dave uncover a Ford Escort thats been untouched for 30 years

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970s Bond Bug three-wheeler divides opinion among Mathewsons customers, while a retired engineer reluctantly auctions off his late fathers treasured 60s Jaguar Mark 2

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: On the fourth leg of his journey through the continent, Simon travels through three of the worlds most extreme environments as he takes in the salt flats of Bolivia, Brazils Pantanal wetland region and Paraguays Chaco Forest. In Bolivia, he meets a family making a living carving salt from the vast white expanse of the Uyuni flats, while in Brazil he has a close encounter with South Americas apex predator, the jaguar. In Paraguay, Simon visits a Mennonites school, where traditionally clothed children are drilled in Bible texts and there are no smartphones to be seen

programme.title: Inside Bentley: A Great British Motorcar
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at the car companys plant as work gets underway on a range of 4x4s, from the factory floor to the worlds most exclusive car showroom

programme.title: Colombia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the country as 50 years of civil war draws to a close in the wake of a recently signed peace deal. He meets guerrilla fighters who have pledged to lay down arms, but also discovers a negative consequence of their disarming, as the paramilitary gangs that control the drugs trade grow in power. Simon travels through the country with coca farmers, who discuss the government support they will need in the turbulent times ahead, as poverty and land ownership becoming pressing issues

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.sub-title: Jowett Javelin and Escort Barn Find
programme.desc: Derek dives into the lost Jowett marque, as a Bradford-built 1952 Javelin finds an unexpected home. Paul and Dave uncover a Ford Escort thats been untouched for 30 years

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevensons Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Cornwalls Tintagel, famed as the legendary birthplace of King Arthur. Ben discovers Tintagel was potentially more important than London a thousand years ago, based on recent evidence unearthed at an archaeological dig overseen by English Heritage. It was found that over 2,000 people were living here which, at the time, which would have made it the largest settlement anywhere of its kind in Britain

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits a small village in Northumberland to find out how the family and village who both share the name Craster have had their lives and fortunes intertwined for over 800 years

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers evidence of the German occupation of the Jersey village of Gorey and the role it played in defending the UK. He meets local historian Phillip Marett, who takes him to the gun tower from where the occupying soldiers could have watched the Battle of Normandy, and hears a first-hand recollection of the liberation of the island

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers how the Cornish village of Botallack was the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry and supplied a deadly poison to the world. Local resident Paula Nicholls explains how her ancestors, the Boyne family, owned a mine which was involved in a catastrophic explosion in 1893 where 20 men tragically died

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The story of the GM Opel Blitz and the American-owned war factories that would produce them, putting business before country

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The return of the documentary, picking up in the immediate aftermath of the Second World War, when the Allies uncovered the scale of the Holocaust. Preparations began for a trial at Nuremberg, where 22 of the highest-ranking Nazis, including Hermann Goering, were to face charges of war crimes and crimes against humanity. But not all the highest-ranking Nazis had been captured, and the job of tracking them down fell to Allied soldiers

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Eltham Palace in Greenwich, south-east London, where members of the public get the chance to have their family treasures assessed by the experts, with Joanna Hardy dazzled by an exquisite emerald and diamond bracelet made by one of Paris top design houses. Also featured are tea set from the ship used by Captain Scott on his ill-fated Antarctic expedition of 1910, a giant thermometer used as an advertisement and a Roman jug thats survived unscathed for 2,000 years

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a compilation of highlights from the past 25 years of the show, looking back at some of the visitors who were surprised how much their possessions were worth. Expert Marc Allum discusses some of his favourite finds, including a tapestry celebrating rock band Status Quo, and a rare camera which sold for a small fortune

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Mathewsons agree to sell a vintage threshing machine and large collection of tractors, but extracting them from old barns on a Yorkshire farm proves a bigger job than anticipated

programme.desc: Derek waxes lyrical about a pristine Jaguar XJ8 and a Ford Model T van tests his driving skills

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcocks films and Graham Greenes books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The explorer digs out his rucksack and heads for Windermere in the Lake District. The National Park attracts 18 million visitors each year, and is now a World Heritage site. Here, Paul finds out why the early tourists thought the Lakes was one of the most dangerous spots in the country. He also plunges into the world of long-distance swimming and helps the staff who run the boats on the lake and meets a Holocaust survivor who was airlifted to Windermere in 1945 as part of a mercy mission involving 300 orphans after the Second World War

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The explorer heads to Derwentwater and Borrowdale, a region of England susceptible to extreme weather, where he meets some of the locals who have battled against flooding and learned to live with the conditions. Along the way, Paul tells the story of Millican Dalton, the Professor of Adventure, who ran his own expeditions in the Lake District more than 70 years ago. He also heads for the summit of Great Gable, one of the Lakes best-known peaks

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Coniston Water isnt the biggest of Cumbrias lakes - but its one of the most iconic and the place where Donald Campbell died trying to break the world water speed record. Paul meets Campbells daughter Gina and crew member Robbie Robinson, who was there on the fateful day in January 1967 when Bluebird somersaulted and crashed on the water. The explorer also enters a Lakeland bike ride with a difference before ending his visit with a spot of rock climbing at Dow Crag before the ascent of one of the areas best known hills, The Old Man of Coniston

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Remote Eskdale is the next destination in the explorers trek through the Lake District, enjoying the landscapes and meeting its people. The corner of the western Lakes he focuses on is off the normal tourist track, yet boasts Englands highest mountain. However, before making the tough climb to the summit of Scafell Pike, Paul meets the folk who run one of the countrys most scenic railways, has a close encounter with a vulture, and gets to grips with the locals in a spot of Cumberland wrestling

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek travels to the Yorkshire coast to pick up a 1963 Austin J2 Paralanian campervan, described as the Rolls Royce of campervans.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Sunderland to pick up a real Italian beauty - a 1972 Lancia Fulvia Zagato. Red and racy, the car has seen better days, but Dereks still impressed

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul and Jack head to Staffordshire to pick up five vehicles from collector Robs colossal barn, including a Mini and a Morris Minor

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves in Lincolnshire picking up a 1935 Riley Falcon - a good-looking saloon car, with a pre-select gearbox

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: An examination of the dark history behind Britains fascination with murder, beginning with historian and curator Lucy Worsley exploring how notorious killings were transformed into popular entertainment in the first half of the 19th century

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Ben Robinson visits Laxey on the Isle of Man to discover what drew Victorians to the tourist destination. It wasnt the beaches and the mountains that attracted Victorian tourists, but an engineering marvel - the biggest waterwheel in the world

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Ben Robinson discovers more about the quiet village of Ravenglass in the Lake Districts National Park, which played a strategic role in the Roman empire. With the help of the Pennington family, whose ancestral home is Muncaster Castle, Ben is shown their latest Roman find, a unique stone slab with a Latin inscription thats been attributed to a Roman naval unit, Aelia Classica

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Yorkshire coastal village of Flamborough and discovers its historic chalk buildings. Local historian and archaeologist Andrew Jones shows Ben around Flamborough Tower. Constructed in 1674 and standing 87 feet tall, the tower was built with the purpose of improving ship to shore navigation but would ultimately be used by local law enforcers in a bid to prevent smuggling

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Norfolk village of Cley next the Sea and finds that due to Dutch settlers in the Elizabethan era, its not as next the sea as it once was. Ben talks to historian Onyeka Nubia, who reveals that the village was part of a cosmopolitan community that introduced Britain to the canary and even the beer people drink today

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The story of how car designer Ferdinand Porsche became one of the most influential engineers in all Nazi Germany

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: By April 1945 most of Germany was occupied by invading Allied forces, with the two-million strong Soviet army just a mile from Hitlers hiding place In Berlin. With the Nazi regime disintegrating, most of Hitlers deputies were busy making plans to try to survive the end of the war and only his most die-hard loyalists stay by his side

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from MediaCityUK on the former Manchester docks, and investigates the history of the citys ship canal, which links it to the Irish Sea. Items brought in to be valued include an early animation machine, a statue of Buddha, a magicians box of tricks, and the heaviest treasure ever featured on the show - a 10-metre Canadian totem pole which has to be craned in

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer pier in Norfolk, where items brought in to be valued include a strange machine labelled Certain Death, the Duke of Wellingtons night cap, a note related to Oliver Cromwell, and letters from both Queen Victoria and John Lennon. Plus, Fiona is challenged to guess the values of three rare pieces of antique jewellery

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: On the fourth leg of his journey through the continent, Simon travels through three of the worlds most extreme environments as he takes in the salt flats of Bolivia, Brazils Pantanal wetland region and Paraguays Chaco Forest. In Bolivia, he meets a family making a living carving salt from the vast white expanse of the Uyuni flats, while in Brazil he has a close encounter with South Americas apex predator, the jaguar. In Paraguay, Simon visits a Mennonites school, where traditionally clothed children are drilled in Bible texts and there are no smartphones to be seen

programme.title: War Factories
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Machine Gun
programme.desc: Hiram Maxim developed the worlds first fully automatic machine gun, but it weighed a whopping 136lbs - until British company Vickers adapted the design

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the foothills of the Himalayas to investigate stories of deadly attacks by the man-eating goonch catfish, tracking down the species to establish if its methods of killing are as gruesome as reports suggest

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Mr Rose and Dr Ormerod attempt to rescue a drowning boy during a trip to a hippie commune. Catherine is asked on a date by a local fisherman, while Dr Goodwin discovers the parents of a sick baby are not all they seem after he diagnoses the infants illness. Robert Daws, Denis Lill, Amelia Curtis and Paul Fox star

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Irelands most decorated cerebral palsy footballer talks to Tommy Martin about his successful career and the personal challenges he overcame

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the jockey, who reflects on a pioneering career, a life immersed in horse racing, and her role as a role model to other women

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, three singletons compete in a series of shopping challenges to win the affections of sweet shop worker Jessica Gill. To impress her, they have to dress her. Jess has no information to go on but the outfits bought, so which of the three competitors will she choose to take on a date?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, three singletons battle it out in a series of shopping challenges to win the affections of car sales executive Chelsea

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the celebrity cookery series where famous faces will be cooking up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Tonight, the remaining four celebrities battle it out for a place in the final, cooking up their best South-east Asian dishes to impress the judges

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the former Leinster and Italy rugby player, who lost sight in his eye following a horrific injury

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Ainsleys National Trust Cook Off
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chefs Serena Appleby and Judy Joo at Tyntesfield in Somerset to see who can create and cook the Dish of the Day this time. Ainsley collects an Ashmeads kernel apple for his maple-griddled apple, fennel and prosciutto salad with sourdough croutons, Judy brings Korean influences to her grilled chicken kale salad with miso carrot ginger dressing, and Serena shows off her summer garden frittata, radish and red onion quickle and fresh herb chimichurri

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The opening part of an interview with the former Republic of Ireland star, as Tommy Martin talks to him about his journey through football from Sallynoggin to Manchester United to the World Cup, and his relationships with iconic managers Jack Charlton and Alex Ferguson

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to Thailand in search of the giant freshwater stingray, one of the worlds largest and most terrifying river fish. Armed with a venomous 10in barb on the end of its whip-like tail, this 700lb predator tests the extreme angler to his physical limits, eventually snapping his rod and ripping his bicep

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: A Labrador has his first day as a drug sniffer, the managers see if they can smuggle a fake bomb past their screening teams, and Annie faces her moment of truth

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.sub-title: From the Grapefruit to the Skinny Gut Diet
programme.desc: Lauren and Robyn from Watford have 12 days to slim down before a 30th birthday party, with one eating half a grapefruit before each calorie-controlled meal and the other going on the apple cider vinegar diet. Two mums from north London want to lose weight for a friends daughters bat mitzvah in six weeks time, so Emma goes on a regimen to balance her gut bacteria, while Elisa takes on the blood-focused Eat Right for Your Type diet, and Carl and Paul in Wigan go on raw and fast food diets. Xand also sheds light on some of the latest dieting trends and diet obsessions, including DIY enemas

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the celebrity cookery series where famous faces will be cooking up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. The remaining celebrities take on French cuisine as they battle to avoid elimination and stay in the competition

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: After a week of people from across the UK willing to take on Jenny The Vixen Ryan, Anne The Governess Hegerty, Shaun The Dark Destroyer Wallace, Mark The Beast Labbett and Paul The Sinnerman Sinha, more contestants come forward hoping to win big money by conquering the Chasers. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Steve from Christchurch, Lloyd from Tenby, Chris from Salisbury and Jackie from Aylesbury take on one of the Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: The Tonight Show
programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Feathers are ruffled among the staff as a new locum arrives at the hospital, while Ormerod treats the same boy twice in one day - but has difficulty acting on his own advice. Carnegie and Catherine struggle to shake off Ken and Alun during their first date at the races, until their full attention is claimed when a jockey falls from his horse on the track. Robert Cavanah and Amelia Curtis star

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The boxer reflects on his life inside and outside of the ring, from growing up as a triplet to the night in 1992 when he became the first Irishman to win an Olympic gold medal in boxing

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, three more singletons hope to win a date with their style savviness. Hot tub salesman Jack has no information to go on but the outfits that have been bought

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three single men in Cardiff try to win the affection of Ben Brown - also known as Mr Gay Wales 2017 - by each buying their potential date an outfit they think he might like

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: In the final of the contest, the three remaining celebrities showcase all of the skills they have learnt as they compete against each other in a bid to be crowned the winner. Hosted by Emma Willis and Tom Allen
